name: 5 Amino 1 MQ
overview: Small-molecule nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT) inhibitor often grouped with peptides in performance/longevity circles despite not being a peptide.
mechanism_of_action: Inhibits NNMT, which can increase cellular nicotinamide availability and may favor higher NAD+ salvage, altered methylation flux, and reduced adipocyte lipogenesis in preclinical models.
research_applications: NNMT inhibition|Adiposity and body-composition studies|Insulin-sensitivity models|NAD+ salvage/metabolic research
dose_range: 50-150 mg/day orally is commonly discussed in nonclinical/self-experiment settings
administration_notes: Usually sold as oral capsules; some research suppliers also sell vials for injection research
fda_status: Not FDA approved
notes_to_developer: Missing on current site. Needs primary-source research fill. References include seed search URLs plus one web source; curate to primary literature before publication.

name: ACTH
overview: Endogenous pituitary peptide hormone that stimulates the adrenal cortex; therapeutic ACTH products are used mainly for diagnostic testing and select inflammatory indications.
mechanism_of_action: Binds melanocortin 2 receptors on adrenal cortical cells to increase cortisol and related steroid synthesis; also has melanocortin-mediated immunomodulatory effects.
research_applications: Adrenal axis testing|Steroidogenesis research|Anti-inflammatory signaling|Pituitary-adrenal physiology
dose_range: Context-dependent; diagnostic cosyntropin testing commonly uses 250 mcg, while repository corticotropin dosing varies by indication
administration_notes: Use depends on formulation; native ACTH is rapidly cleared while depot products last longer
half_life: Native ACTH only minutes in plasma; depot formulations act longer
fda_status: Approved formulations exist, but usage depends on exact product
notes_to_developer: Missing on current site. Needs primary-source research fill. References include seed search URLs plus one web source; curate to primary literature before publication.

name: Adamax

overview: Synthetic dipeptide (Glu-Trp) that mimics thymic peptide activity.

name: Thymosin Alpha-1
overview: 28-amino acid thymic peptide that activates dendritic cell maturation via TLR9, promotes T-cell differentiation (Th1 polarization), enhances NK cell cytotoxicity, and modulates inflammatory cytokine balance.

name: Tirzepatide
overview: Dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist (twincretin).
mechanism_of_action: Dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist (twincretin). Activates both incretin pathways for synergistic insulin secretion, glucagon suppression, and appetite reduction. Superior weight loss versus GLP-1 mono-agonists in clinical trials.
research_applications: Diabetes and obesity research|GIP/GLP-1 dual agonist pharmacology|Weight-loss outcomes|Cardiometabolic studies
fda_status: Research only / status to be verified

name: Triptorelin
overview: Long-acting GnRH agonist used for prostate cancer, endometriosis, precocious puberty, IVF protocols, and related endocrine indications.
mechanism_of_action: Initially stimulates LH/FSH release, then downregulates pituitary GnRH receptors with continuous exposure, suppressing gonadal steroid production.
research_applications: Fertility and puberty research|Medical castration/endometriosis protocols|GnRH receptor pharmacology|Reproductive endocrinology
dose_range: Product-specific depot regimens vary from monthly to multi-month injections; short-acting IVF use differs
break_before_continuing: Not typically cycled outside fertility protocols
administration_notes: Depot IM/SC formulations are common in oncology and gynecology; IVF uses separate protocols
half_life: Hours for short-acting drug; depot exposure persists weeks to months
fda_status: Approved
notes_to_developer: Missing on current site. Needs primary-source research fill. References include seed search URLs plus one web source; curate to primary literature before publication.

name: VIP
overview: Vasoactive intestinal peptide is an endogenous neuropeptide with potent vasodilatory, bronchodilatory, and immunomodulatory effects.
mechanism_of_action: Signals primarily through VPAC1/VPAC2 receptors to increase cAMP, relax smooth muscle, alter cytokine balance, and modulate secretory functions.
research_applications: Bronchodilation and vasodilation research|Immune modulation|Gut-lung axis studies|Neuropeptide signaling
dose_range: No standardized broad therapeutic dose; research and specialty-clinic intranasal/injectable protocols vary
administration_notes: Most specialty use is intranasal or injectable; peptide is rapidly degraded systemically
half_life: Very short in circulation, often only minutes
fda_status: Not FDA approved as a general peptide therapy
